name: azure-ai-voicelive-ts
description: Azure AI Voice Live SDK for JavaScript and TypeScript. Build real-time voice AI applications with bidirectional WebSocket communication. Use for voice assistants, conversational AI, real-time speech-to-speech, and voice-enabled chatbots in Node.js or browser environments.
capabilities: Create VoiceLiveClient instances for Azure AI Voice Live service
Configure real-time voice sessions with WebSocket communication
Set up event handlers for audio streaming and transcription
Implement function calling with voice AI assistants
Configure voice options including Azure Standard, Custom, and OpenAI voices
Handle turn detection with server VAD or Azure Semantic VAD
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing guidance for using the Azure AI Voice Live SDK. No executable code was detected. The skill provides usage examples for a legitimate Azure service. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: This skill provides comprehensive documentation and code examples for building real-time voice AI applications using the Azure AI Voice Live SDK. It enables developers to create voice assistants, conversational AI, and speech-to-speech applications in JavaScript and TypeScript.
userTitle: Build Real-Time Voice AI Apps with Azure
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and valid credentials (Entra ID or API key)
Only supports specific audio formats (PCM16, G711)
Depends on network connectivity for real-time communication
Browser usage requires bundler setup (Vite, webpack)

useCases.title: Build Voice Assistants
useCases.description: Create interactive voice assistants that can understand speech, respond with AI-generated audio, and handle multi-turn conversations.

useCases.title: Real-Time Transcription
useCases.description: Implement live speech-to-text transcription with low latency for customer service, accessibility, or documentation applications.

useCases.title: Conversational Chatbots
useCases.description: Build voice-enabled chatbots that can have natural spoken conversations with users using GPT models.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Voice Client Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up a basic VoiceLiveClient using Microsoft Entra ID authentication in TypeScript.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Azure AI Voice Live

promptTemplates.title: Session Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure a voice session with text and audio modalities, custom instructions, and Azure Semantic VAD turn detection.

promptTemplates.title: Event Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement event handlers for streaming audio delta, text delta, and transcription events using the subscription pattern.

promptTemplates.title: Function Calling
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up function calling tools in the session configuration and handle function call events to integrate external APIs.

bestPractices: Always use DefaultAzureCredential instead of hardcoding API keys for secure authentication
Use Azure Semantic VAD for better turn detection than basic server VAD
Clean up subscriptions by calling subscription.close() when done to prevent memory leaks
antiPatterns: Hardcoding API keys directly in source code instead of using environment variables or Entra ID
Not handling connection, authentication, and protocol errors separately
Setting only audio modality without text - this breaks many conversational features

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: Microsoft Entra ID (recommended) and API key authentication using AzureKeyCredential.

faq.question: What environments are supported?
faq.answer: Node.js LTS (20+) and modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) with a bundler.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: PCM16 at 24kHz (default), PCM16-8kHz, PCM16-16kHz, G711 ulaw, and G711 alaw.

faq.question: How does turn detection work?
faq.answer: Server VAD uses voice activity detection. Azure Semantic VAD uses AI to understand conversational context for better turn-taking.

faq.question: Can I use custom voices?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Custom voices with endpoint ID and Azure Personal voices for speaker profile cloning are supported.

faq.question: What models are supported?
faq.answer: GPT-4o-realtime-preview, GPT-4o-mini-realtime-preview, and phi4-mm-realtime for cost-effective applications.
